Person preferred name
The name by which a person wishes to be addressed. The preferred name volunteered by the person or a preferred name given by PDS that the person has asked to be called by.

Immigration status
Include details of the immigration status of the person, their permissions to live and receive care and support in the UK e.g. asylum seeker, indefinite leave to remain in the UK. Include start date, end date and any comments.
R 0…1
Immigration status
Details of the immigration status of the person, their permissions to live and receive care and support in the UK e.g. asylum seeker, indefinite leave to remain in the UK.
R 0…1
PLACEHOLDER — Code :- 1: Asylum Seeker; 2: Exceptional leave to remain in UK; 3: Indefinite Leave to Remain in UK ;4: Limited Leave to Remain in UK; 5: No Recourse to Public Funds (NRPF); 6: Other Nationals; 7: Refugee; 8: Unaccompanied Asylum Seeker; 9 EU pre-settled status; 10: EU settled status (Example)
Start date
Start date of immigration status
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
End date
End date of immigration status
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
Comment
Relevant comments about the person's immigration status
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Organ and tissue donation
Whether the person has given consent for organ and/or tissue donation or opted out of automatic donation where applicable. This is information provided by the national register.
R 0…1
boolean
PLACEHOLDER — Flag: - Yes or No (Example)
Overseas visitor status
The charging category relating to an overseas visitor.

GP practice

Details of the person's GP practice.

M 1…1


GP practice record entry

This is a GP practice record entry. There may be 0 to many record entries under a section. Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items.

R 0…*


Record
GP name

The name of the person's GP. If the person is registered with a GP practice, their usual GP name will be something volunteered by the person or their representative.

R 0…1


Person Name
GP practice details

Name and address of the person's registered GP Practice. Registered GP practice details are available from the Person Demographics Service (PDS), or volunteered from the person or their representative. Include details of the practice name and address.

R 0…1


Address
GP practice identifier

The identifier of the registered GP practice.

M 1…1


http://snomed.info/sct — This is another test value set. It should be under binding. (Example)
About me

About me

R 0…1


What is most important to me
A description of what is most important to you.

R 0…1
What is most important to me
A description of what is most important to you 

Emergency Information : -  Include any essential information that any professional in health and social care should know about the you in any situation, including emergencies.
Other Information : - This could include : - Values, Spirituality/religion, Ethnicity, Culture, Pets, Goals and aspirations, Meaningful activities including leisure activities, visiting places, sport and exercise, listening to music, employment, education, volunteering
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
People who are important to me
Details of who is important to you and why. 
R 0…1
People who are important to me
Details of who is important to you and why. They could be family members, carers, friends, members of staff etc. Include how you want the people important to you to be engaged and involved in your care and support in both emergency and normal situations. Include how you stay connected to the people important to you. Who should not be contacted or consulted about your care and support and why, if you wish to say
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
How I communicate and how to communicate with me
A description of how you communicate normally. 
R 0…1
How I communicate and how to communicate with me

A description of how you communicate normally including any communication aids you use, for example a hearing aid.

  • Include your preferred language of communication, if your first language is not English.
  • Include how you would communicate when you are in pain or distress.
  • Include how you communicate choices.
  • Include how you give feedback or raise a concern.
  • Include how you like to receive information

Describe how you would like others to engage and communicate with you, including how you would like to be addressed.

R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
My wellness
A description covering what you are able to do, how you engage with others and how you feel on a typical day through to on a day when you are unwell or really unwell.
R 0…1
My wellness

A description covering what you are able to do, how you engage with others and how you feel on a typical day through to on a day when you are unwell or really unwell

  • Include any causes that might result in you becoming unwell and strategies for avoiding or addressing the causes. For example, not drinking enough water could cause constipation.
  • Include any signs that indicate you might be becoming unwell.
  • On a bad day describe what is different about what you are able to do, how you engage with others and how you feel.
  • Include how your everyday life is affected by any medical conditions e.g. dementia and any symptoms e.g. itchiness, cough, pain and how you manage those conditions.
  • Include past health issues or experiences that need to be considered
  • Include your wellbeing and lifestyle goals and aspirations

R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Please do and please don't
A description of things you want someone supporting you to do (or not to do). 
R 0…1
Please do and please don't
A description of things you want someone supporting you to do (or not to do).
Things you want someone to  do  might include (for example): - Talk to me not to my carer, remind me to take my medication, encourage me to wash my hands regularly, explain to me what is happening and why, respond to my communication.
A description of things you  do not  want someone supporting you to do might include (for example): - Discussing or asking questions about certain topics, making assumptions about something, providing support when it is not wanted, talking to you in a certain way, undergoing a specific intervention or taking a medication the person does not want.

R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
How and when to support me
A description of how and when you want someone caring for you to support you.
R 0…1
How and when to support me

A description of how and when you want someone caring for you to support you.

  • This could include support needs in an emergency situation (for example taking blood)
  • This could include support you need to maintain important routines or to carry out particular activities, for example: - Personal care routines, Eating and drinking, Bedtime routines, Taking medications, Moving and transitioning
  • This could also include support needed with:- wearing glasses/hearing aids/false teeth etc, making informed choices or understanding dangers and risks, managing your emotions, moods and behaviours, memory or confusion.
  • Include how your support needs change in different environments. Include any triggers that might result in you needing further support and strategies for avoiding or addressing the triggers. Include how you want the support to be provided.

R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Also worth knowing about me
A description of what is also worth knowing about you for people caring or supporting you. 
R 0…1
Also worth knowing about me

A description of what is also worth knowing about you for people caring or supporting you.

  • This could include a short history of your life (where you have worked, where you lived, important events in your life, important people in your past life).
  • This could include a short profile of your current life:- your work / study, your aspirations, your skills, your networks, things you like e.g. particular foods, places, a football team and things you like to talk about, things you dislike
  • This could also include any care and support preferences that have not been included elsewhere.

R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Date
This is a record of the date that this information was last updated.
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and Time (Example)
Supported to write this by
Where relevant, this is a record of name, relationship/role and contact details of the individual who supported the person to write this section e.g. carer, family member, advocate, professional.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Location to multimedia file
The link to the person's multimedia file
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Link to URL (Example)
Individual requirements

The individual requirements of the person.

R 0…1


Reasonable adjustment
A record of reasonable adjustments that must be provided by the service to comply with the Equality Act 2010.
R 0…1
Reasonable adjustment record entry
This is a reasonable adjustment record entry. There may be 0 to many record entries under a section. Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items
R 0…*
Location
The location where the reasonable adjustment was identified.
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for location.
R 0…1
string
NHS Data Dictionary — Organisation data service code (Example)
Free text
Free text field to be used if no code is available
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Reasonable adjustment flag

Under the Equality Act 2010 , organisations have a legal duty to make changes in their approach or provision to ensure that services are as accessible to people with disabilities as they are for everybody else. These changes are called reasonable adjustments.

Reasonable adjustments can mean alterations to buildings by providing lifts, wide doors, ramps and tactile signage, but may also mean changes to policies, procedures and staff training to ensure that services work equally well for people with physical or sensory disabilities, learning disabilities or long-term conditions such as dementia.

Other adjustments include longer appointments, providing easy read materials or communications via a carer. This legal duty is anticipatory which means a service should know about a person’s need for adjustments when they are referred or present for care. For this to happen, and for optimum care to be delivered, adjustments need to be recorded and shared across the NHS.

R 0…1
boolean
SNOMED CT — SNOMED CT: - 1108111000000107 |Requires reasonable adjustments for health and care access (Equality Act 2010) (finding)| (Example)
Reasonable adjustment
The reasonable adjustment required e.g. requires specific contact method, requires communication professional
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for reasonable adjustment
R 0…1

Free text
Free text field to be used if no code is available
R 0…1

text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Impairment additional detail

Description of what the person can do with the impairment, what they want to be able to do and how they should be supported to do it and what the consequences are of not providing the support.
Where there is a sensory disability include sensory processing difficulties (e.g. affects to balance and movement, oversensitive, under-sensitive).

Alerts

Details of alerts.

0…1


Alerts record entry
This is an alerts record entry. There may be 0 to many record entries under a section. Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items
0…*
Alert
Any significant information meriting a specific and highly visible warning to any user (e.g. metallic implant, potential dangerous pet).

string
NHS Data Dictionary — Organisation data service code (Example)

R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Consent for information sharing
This is a record of consent for information sharing under the common law duty of confidentiality. Where consent has not been obtained or sought, the reason why should be provided. Include best interests decision where person lacks capacity. This is a placeholder until the national guidance is published.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Reason for consent not given
Reason not given for consent
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Performing professional
The professional who took the consent for information sharing

Name
The name of the professional.
R 0…1

text
Consent relating to child
The person with parental responsibility or appointed guardian where a child lacks competency. Consideration of age and competency, applying Gillick competency or Fraser guidelines.

Mental capacity assessment
Whether an assessment of the mental capacity of the (adult) person has been undertaken, if so, what capacity the decision relates to and the outcome of the assessment. Also record best interests decision if person lacks capacity.

Lasting power of attorney for health and welfare or court-appointed deputy (or equivalent)
Details of the person's LPA record or equivalent
R 0…*
Date
The date the LPA was recorded
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
Location
The location where the LPA was given
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for location
R 0…1
string
NHS Data Dictionary — Organisation code (Example)
Free text
Free text field to be used if no code is available
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Lasting power of attorney for health and welfare or court appointed deputy (or equivalent)
Record of one or more people who have been given power (LPA) by the person when they had capacity to make decisions about their health and welfare should they lose capacity to make those decisions. To be valid, an LPA must have been registered with the Court of Protection. If life-sustaining treatment is being considered the LPA document must state specifically that the attorney has been given power to consent to or refuse life-sustaining treatment. Details of any person (deputy) appointed by the court to make decisions about the person’s health and welfare. A deputy does not have the power to refuse life-sustaining treatment.
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for the LPA
R 0…1
SNOMED CT — SNOMED CT: - ^999001951000000107 |Personal welfare lasting power of attorney findings simple reference set (foundation metadata concept)| (Example)
Location of document
The location of the LPA information
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text or URL (Example)
Name of LPA
The names of any appointed people or deputies
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Contact details
The contact details of the LPA
R 0…1

NHS Data Dictionary — UK telephone number (Example)
Performing professional
The professional who took the details of the LPA or equivalent

Advance decision to refuse treatment (ADRT)
Details of the person's ADRT status.
R 0…1
Date
The date the ADRT was recorded.
R 0…1
date
PLACEHOLDER — Date (Example)
Location
The location where the ADRT was recorded.
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value of location
R 0…1
string
NHS Data Dictionary — Organisation data service (Example)
Free text
Free text field to be used if no coded text available
R 0…1
text
Advance decision to refuse treatment
A record of an advance decision to refuse one or more specific types of future treatment, made by a person who had capacity at the time of recording the decision. The decision only applies when the person no longer has the capacity to consent to or refuse the specific treatment being considered. An ADRT must be in writing, signed and witnessed. If the ADRT is refusing life-sustaining treatment it must state specifically that the treatment is refused even if the person’s life is at risk.
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for advance decision to refuse treatment.
R 0…1
SNOMED CT — 999002181000000105 (Example)
Free text
Free text field to be used if no code is available
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Performing professional
The professional who recorded the ADRT

The name of the professional.

Professional contacts

The details of the person’s professional contacts.

R 0…1


Professional contacts record entry
This is a professional contacts record entry. There may be 0 to many record entries under a section. Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items.
R 0…*
Name
The name of the professional with responsibility for the care of the person.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Role
The role the professional has in relation to the person e.g. GP, physiotherapist, community nurse, social worker, key worker, care home manager, care coordinator, LA hospital liaison person, care home contact, hospital clinician, Independent Mental Capacity Advocate (IMCA) etc
R 0…1
FHIR — SDSjobrole (Example)
Key worker
A flag that identifies the personal contact is the key worker assigned to the person.
R 0…1
boolean
PLACEHOLDER — Flag : - Yes or No (Example)
Speciality
The specialty of the professional e.g. physiotherapy, oncology, mental health etc
R 0…1
string
NHS Data Dictionary — Activity treatment function code (Example)
Team
The name of the team.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Organisation
The name of the organisation.
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for location
R 0…1
string
NHS Data Dictionary — Organisation data service (Example)
Free text
Free text field to be used if no code is available
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Contact details
Contact details of the professional (e.g. telephone number, email address etc.).
R 0…*
string
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Start date
The start date of the professional relationship with the person.
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
End date
The end date of the professional relationship with the person.
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
Personal contacts

The details of the individual's personal contacts.

R 0…1


Personal contacts record entry
This is personal contacts record entry. There may be 0 to many record entries under a section. Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items.
R 0…*
Name
The name of the personal contact.
M 1…1
string
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Relationship
The relationship the personal contact has to the person, e.g. father, grandmother, family friend etc.
R 0…1
FHIR — Related person (Example)
Relationship type
Additional roles the person performs on behalf of the other (e.g. Carer, Next of Kin, Emergency Contact, Dependent, etc.)
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Contact details
Contact details of the personal contact (e.g. telephone number, email address etc.)
R 0…*
string
PLACEHOLDER — Free Text (Example)
Comments
Notes on the significance of the personal contact to the person.
0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)

text

Referral details

The details of the referral.

R 0…1


Referral details record entry
This is the referral details record entry. There may be 0 to many record entries under a section. Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items.
R 0…*
Date

The date of referral

R 0…1


Date Time
Referrer details
The details of the referrer that is making the referral or the transfer.

This could be the person, GP surgery, department, specialty, sub-specialty, educational institution, mental health team, care home etc.

R 0…1
Name

The name of the referrer.

R 0…1


Person Name
Role

Role of the referrer.

R 0…1


Coded Element
FHIR — sdsjobrole (Example)
Grade

The grade of the referrer.

R 0…1


String
Team

The team or department of the referrer.

R 0…1


String
Specialty

The specialty of the referrer.

R 0…1


Coded Element
NHS Data Dictionary — Activity treatment function code (Example)
Service

The service of the referrer.

R 0…1


Coded Element
Organisation

The organisation of the referrer.

R 0…1


Coded Element
NHS Data Dictionary — Organisation (Example)
Contact details

The contact details of the referrer.

R 0…1


String
Referral method

The method in which a referral is sent and received. This may be a letter, email, transcript of a telephone conversation, Choose and Book, in person (self-referral) etc.

R 0…1


Coded Element
Reason for referral

The reason for the referral, e.g. diagnosis, treatment, transfer of care due to relocation, investigation, second opinion, management of the patient (e.g. palliative care), or carer's concerns.

R 0…1


Coded Element
Clinical urgency of referral
The details of the clinical urgency of referral
R 0…1
Urgency
This is the priority of a request for services
R 0…1
string
NHS Data Dictionary — PRIORITY TYPE (Example)
Reason for urgency of referral
The reason for urgency of referral if not routine.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Referral to
Details of where the referral is to be sent.

If not an individual, this could be a service, e.g. GP surgery, department, specialty, subspecialty, educational institution, mental health etc.

R 0…1
Name

Name of person the referral is to be sent to.

R 0…1


Person Name
Role

Role of person where the referral is to be sent to.

R 0…1


Coded Element
FHIR — sdsjobrole (Example)
Grade

The grade of the person where the referral is sent to.

R 0…1


String
Team

The team or department of the person where the referral is being sent to

R 0…1


String
Specialty

The specialty the referral is being sent to e.g. physiotherapy, oncology, mental health etc

R 0…1


Coded Element
Service
The service of where the referral is sent to.
R 0…1
Organisation
The organisation of where the referral is to be sent.
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for organisation
R 0…1
string
NHS Data Dictionary — Organisation data service (Example)
Free text
Free text field to be used if no code is available
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Contact details

The contact details of where the referral is to be sent.

R 0…1


String
Referral criteria

Records whether specific criteria required for referral, to a particular service, have been met (may be nationally or locally determined). This field is used to outline which criteria have been met where a service has indicated specific criteria and to explain the rationale for referral where criteria have not been met.

R 0…1


Text
Return response to

Name of professional to be communicated with, if not the referrer

R 0…1


Person Name
Contacts with professionals

The details of the person’s contact with a professional.

decimal
PLACEHOLDER — Numeric value (Example)

PLACEHOLDER — Flag: - Yes or No (Example)

Reported

R 0…1
Date
The date or partial date that the reported vaccination was given in the opinion of the person.
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date/time (Example)
Reported
A flag to indicate the information was reported to a professional
R 0…1
boolean
PLACEHOLDER — Flag: - Yes or No (Example)
Report origin

R 0…1
string
FHIR — FHIR value set:- https://www.hl7.org/fhir/valueset-immunization-origin.html (Example)
Problem list

A summary of the problems that require investigation or treatment.

R 0…1


Problem list record entry
This is a problem list record entry.

There may be 0 to many record entries under problem list.

Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items.



R 0…*
Date
The date the problem was identified.
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)

The professional who identified the problem.

PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Problem
A condition which needs addressing and so is important for every professional to know about when seeing the person.

Problems may include diagnoses, symptoms, disabilities and social or behavioural issues

M 1…1
Coded value
The coded value for the problem list.
R 0…1

Procedures and therapies

The details of any procedures performed. Includes both psychological and medical therapies and procedures (e.g. cognitive behaviour therapy, hip replacement)

R 0…1


Procedure record entry
This is a procedure record entry. There may be 0 to many record entries under a section. Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items.
R 0…*
Date
The date the procedure was performed.
R 0…1

Procedure
The therapeutic or diagnostic procedure performed. Includes both psychological and medical therapies and procedures (e.g. cognitive behaviour therapy, or follow-up interventions as a result of physical health checks). Complementary or alternative procedures and therapies should be recorded here.
M 1…1
Coded value
The procedure code.
R 0…1

Services and care

The services and care provided for the person.

R 0…1


Services and care record entry
This is a services and care record entry. There may be 0 to many record entries under a section. Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items.
R 0…*
Social care package name
Name of specific type of package being provided (e.g. Reablement, Home Based Care XYZ, etc)
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Social care package type
Higher level categorisation of package type, e.g. Adults Home Support
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Service start date
The date service started to be provided.
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
Service end date
The date the service is planned to be ended or the actual end date.
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
Local authority
The name of the local authority providing the care package.
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for local authority
R 0…1
string

Free text
Free text field to be used if no code is available
R 0…1

Primary support reason

The primary support reason for social care.

R 0…1


Primary support reason record entry
This is a primary support reason record entry. There may be 0 to many record entries under a section. Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items.
R 0…*
Primary support reason
The Primary Support Reason used to classify a person for National Reporting on Short and Long Term Services (SALT) e.g. Learning disability support, mental health support, physical support
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Active
Flag to indicate whether the primary support reason is currently active
R 0…1
boolean
PLACEHOLDER — Flag: - Yes or No (Example)
Start date
The date a primary support reason was authorised as active.
R 0…1
date
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
End date
The end date of authorisation.
R 0…1
date
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
Family history

Family history

R 0…1


Family history record entry
This is a family history record entry. There may be 0 to many record entry/entries under a section. Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items.
R 0…1

The date the family history was taken.
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
Location
The location where the family history was taken.

Relationship to person
The relationship of the family member with the condition to the person.
R 0…1

The coded value for relationship to person
R 0…1
SNOMED CT — SNOMED CT: - <303071001 |Person in the family (person)| (Example)
Free text
Free text field to be used if no code is available
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Family history
The record of relevant illness in family relations deemed to be significant to the care or health of the person, including mental illness and suicide, genetic information etc.
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for family history
R 0…1
SNOMED CT — SNOMED CT:- <404684003 |Clinical finding (finding)| OR <243796009 |Situation with explicit context (situation)| (Example)
Free text
Free text field to be used if no coded text available

Role
The role the professional has in relation to the person e.g. GP, physiotherapist, community nurse, social worker etc

Observations
The record of essential physiological measurements, e.g., heart rate, blood pressure, temperature, pulse, height, weight, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation.
R 0…1
Observations record entry
This is an observations record entry. There may be 0 to many record entry/entries under a section. Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items.
R 0…*
Coded value
The coded value for observations
R 0…1
SNOMED CT — SNOMED CT: - <363787002 | Observable entity (observable entity) | (Example)
Value
The measured value of the observations
R 0…1
quantity
PLACEHOLDER — Enumerated value e.g. numeric, natural numbers, mild, moderate (Example)
Units of measure
The units of measure for the observation.
R 0…1
string
UCUM — UCUM code (Example)
Site
The anatomical site at which the observation is taken
R 0…1

Interpretation
The assessment made based on the result of the observation e.g high, low

Formulation

Allergies and adverse reactions

Allergies and adverse reactions

R 0…1


Allergies and adverse reactions record entry
This is a allergies and adverse reactions record entry. There may be 1 to many record entries under a section.

Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items.

R 0…*
Type of reaction
The type of reaction experienced by the person (allergic, adverse, intolerance)
R 0…1
FHIR — Allergy, Intolerance (Example)
Causative agent
The agent such as food, drug or substances that has caused or may cause an allergy, intolerance or adverse reaction in this person Or “No known drug allergies or adverse reactions” Or “Information not available”
M 1…1
Coded value
The coded value for causative agent
R 0…1
dm+d — dm+d:- any code from the VTM, VMP, AMP, VMPP, AMPP and ingredient concept classes SNOMED CT : - <105590001 |Substance OR <373873005 |Pharmaceutical / biologic product| OR <<716186003 |No known allergy| OR 196461000000101 |Transfer-degraded drug allergy| OR 196471000000108 |Transfer-degraded non-drug allergy) (Example)
Free text
Free text field to be used if no code is available
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Reaction details cluster
Details of the reaction.
R 0…*
Date
The date that the reaction was identified.
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
Location
Details of where the allergy was identified. Linked to the date of the diagnosis by the professional.
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for location.
R 0…1
string
NHS Data Dictionary — Organisation data service (Example)
Free text
Free text field to be used if no code is available
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Substance
The substance, or a class of substances, that is considered to be responsible for the adverse reaction.
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for substance
R 0…1
SNOMED CT — SNOMED CT : - <105590001 | Substance (substance) | or <373873005 | Pharmaceutical / biologic product (product) | (Example)
Free text
Free text field to be used if no code is available
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Description of reaction
A description of the manifestation of the allergic or adverse reaction experienced by the person. For example, skin rash.
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for description of reaction.
R 0…1
SNOMED CT — SNOMED CT: - ^1127581000000103 |Health issues simple reference set| (Example)
Free text
Free text field to be used if no code is available
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Onset date
Date and time when manifestations showed e.g could be at home a few days earlier than the contact
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
Severity
A description of the severity of the reaction.
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for severity
R 0…1
FHIR — Severity (Example)
Free text
Free text field to be used if no code is available
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Certainty
A description of the certainty that the stated causative agent caused the allergic or adverse reaction.
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for certainty.
R 0…1
FHIR — Certainty (Example)
Free text
Free text field to be used if no code is available
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Route of exposure
The route the person was exposed to the substance
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for route of exposure
R 0…1
SNOMED CT — SNOMED CT: - ^999000051000001100 |ePrescribing route of administration simple reference set| (Example)
Free text

R 0…1
text
Comment
Any additional comment or clarification about the adverse reaction.
0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Evidence
Results of investigations that confirmed the certainty of the diagnosis. Examples might include results of skin prick allergy tests
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Date first experienced
When the reaction was first experienced i.e this may not be the first this has happened. May be a date or partial date (e.g. year) or text ( e.g. during childhood )
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
Allergy end date
The date the allergy was diagnosed as ended
R 0…1
date
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
Comment
Any further comments on the person's allergies or adverse reactions.
0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Performing professional
The professional who identified the reaction.

The name of the professional.

R 0…1
text
Over the counter medication flag
Indicates if the medication or device is acquired without a prescription i.e. got by the person over the counter
R 0…1
boolean
PLACEHOLDER — Flag : - Yes or No (Example)
Structured dose direction cluster
A structural representation of the elements carried by the dose syntax in 'parsable dose strength / timing' i.e. dose strength, dose timing, dose duration and maximum dose.
R 0…1
Dose type
The kind of rate or dose specified 
R 0…1
boolean
PLACEHOLDER — Calculated or ordered (Example)
Structured dose amount
A structural representation of dose amount, e.g. 20mg or 2 tablets. This element will generally only be used when persisting data within systems with 'parsable dose directions' being used to exchange the same information between systems. As per FHIR Dose Syntax Implementation Guidance (NHS Digital):  https://simplifier.net/guide/DoseSyntaxImplementationGuidanceforFHIRR4/Home
R 0…1
Dose
Amount of dose medication .Note that this specifies the quantity of the specified medication, not the quantity for each active ingredient(s). Each ingredient amount can be communicated in the Medication resource. For example, if one wants to communicate that a tablet was 375 mg, where the dose was one tablet, you can use the Medication resource to document that the tablet was comprised of 375 mg of drug XYZ. Alternatively if the dose was 375 mg, then you may only need to use the Medication resource to indicate this was a tablet. If the example were an IV such as dopamine and you wanted to communicate that 400mg of dopamine was mixed in 500 ml of some IV solution, then this would all be communicated in the Medication resource. If the administration is not intended to be instantaneous (rate is present or timing has a duration), this can be specified to convey the total amount to be administered over the period of time as indicated by the schedule e.g. 500 ml in dose, with timing used to convey that this should be done over 4 hours.
R 0…1
quantity
PLACEHOLDER — Numeric (Example)
Rate
Amount of medication per unit of time. Identifies the speed with which the medication was or will be introduced into the patient. Typically the rate for an infusion e.g. 100 ml per 1 hour or 100 ml/hr. May also be expressed as a rate per unit of time e.g. 500 ml per 2 hours. Other examples: 200 mcg/min or 200 mcg/1 minute; 1 liter/8 hours. Sometimes, a rate can imply duration when expressed as total volume / duration (e.g. 500mL/2 hours implies a duration of 2 hours). However, when rate doesn't imply duration (e.g. 250mL/hour), then the timing.repeat.duration is needed to convey the infuse over time period.
R 0…1
decimal
PLACEHOLDER — Numeric (Example)
Structured dose timing
A slot containing a structural, computable representation of dose timing and maximum dose. This element will generally only be used when persisting data within systems with 'parsable dose directions' being used to exchange the same information between systems. As per FHIR Dose Syntax Implementation Guidance (NHS Digital):  https://simplifier.net/guide/DoseSyntaxImplementationGuidanceforFHIRR4/Home
R 0…1
quantity
PLACEHOLDER — Numeric (Example)
Dose direction duration
Recommendation of the time period for which the medication should be continued, including direction not to discontinue. As per FHIR Dose Syntax Implementation Guidance (NHS Digital):  https://simplifier.net/guide/DoseSyntaxImplementationGuidanceforFHIRR4/Home
R 0…1
quantity
PLACEHOLDER — Numeric (Example)
Dose directions description
Describes the entire medication dosage and administration directions including dose quantity and medication frequency and optionally duration e.g. “1 tablet at night" or “2mg at 10pm”.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Maxdoseperperiod
The maximum total quantity of a therapeutic substance that may be administered to a subject over the period of time. For example, 1000mg in 24 hours.
R 0…1
quantity
PLACEHOLDER — Numeric (Example)
Maxdoseperadministration
The maximum total quantity of a therapeutic substance that may be administered to a subject per administration.
R 0…1
quantity
PLACEHOLDER — Numeric (Example)
Repeat medication
Is the medication prescribed to be repeated
R 0…1
Number of repeat prescriptions
The number of repeated prescriptions
R 0…1
decimal
PLACEHOLDER — Numeric (Example)
Review date
Date to review the repeat medication prescribed to the person
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
Additional instructions
Allows for: requirements for adherence support, e.g. compliance aids, prompts and packaging requirements, additional information about specific medicines e.g. where specific brand required, person requirements, e.g. unable to swallow tablets.
R 0…1
FHIR — Additional instruction (Example)
Course details cluster
Details of the overall course of medication.
R 0…1
Course status
The status of this prescription.
R 0…1
FHIR — Medication status (Example)
Indication
Reason for medication being prescribed, where known.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Start date/time
The date and/or time that the medication course should begin.
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
End date/time
The date and/or time that the medication course should finish.
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
Link to indication record
A link to the record which contains the indication for this medication order.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text with URL (Example)
Comment/recommendation
Suggestions about duration and/or review, ongoing monitoring requirements, advice on starting, discontinuing or changing medication. Additional comment or recommendation about the medication course e.g. 'Patient named supply', 'unlicensed medication', 'Foreign brand' or monitoring recommendations
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Medication change summary cluster
Records the changes made to medication.

Status
The nature of any change made to the medication.

Indication
Reason for change in medication, e.g. sub-therapeutic dose, person intolerant.

Plan and requested actions

The details of planned investigations, procedures and treatment, and whether this plan has been agreed with the person or their legitimate representative.

R 0…1


Plan and requested actions record entry
This is a plan and requested actions record entry. There may be 0 to many record entries under a section. Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items.
R 0…*
Date
The date on which the plan and requested actions were prepared.
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)

The name of the professional.

PLACEHOLDER — Free text. (Example)

text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)

R 0…1
string

R 0…1
string
NHS Data Dictionary — Organisation data service (Example)
Free text
Free text field to be used if no code is available
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Actions for professionals
Including planned investigations, procedures and treatment for a person's identified conditions and priorities. For each action the following should be identified:outcome expectations, including the person’s expectations. A record of the planned and requested actions. May be structured (table), with actions, names, dates, status, location, strategies.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Actions for person or their carer
For each action the following should be identified:outcome expectations, including person’s expectations. A record of the planned and requested actions. May be structured (table), with actions, names, dates, status, location, strategies.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Agreed with the person or their legitimate representative
Indicates whether the person or their legitimate representative has agreed the entire plan or individual aspects of treatment, expected outcomes, risks and alternative treatments. A record of the agreement of the decisions made.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Outcome of plan and requested actions
The details of the outcome of the plan and requested actions.
R 0…*
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text. (Example)

Care and support plan

This records the decisions reached during conversation between the individual and health and care professional about future plans and also records progress.

R 0…1


Strengths
Any strengths and assets the person has relating to their goals and hopes about their health and well-being.
R 0…*
Coded value
The coded value associated with the person's strengths.  Could be derived from the assessment of the person e.g. 300738008 |Able to participate in leisure activities (finding)|



R 0…1
SNOMED CT — SNOMED CT : - 1127601000000107 | Healthcare matters simple reference set (foundation metadata concept) | (Example)
Free text
Any supplementary or personalised information that relates to the person's strengths.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Needs, concerns or problems
Details of the person needs, concerns or problems

R 0…*
Coded value
The coded value associated with the person's needs, concerns or problems.
R 0…1
SNOMED CT — ^1127581000000103 |Health issues simple reference set| (Example)
Free text
Any supplementary or personalised information that relates to the person's needs, concerns and problems.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Goals and hopes
The overall goals, hopes, aims or targets that the person has. Including anything that the person wants to achieve that relates to their future health and wellbeing. Each goal may include a description of why it is important to the person. Goals may also be ranked in order of importance or priority to the person.
R 0…*
Coded value
The coded value associated with the person's goals, aim's and hopes. e.g. 999002111000000103 |Occupational therapy goals simple reference set (foundation metadata concept)|
R 0…1
SNOMED CT — SNOMED CT : - 413350009|Finding with explicit context|: 246090004|Associated finding|=300798006 |Able to participate in sporting activities (finding)|, 408729009|Finding context|=410518001|Goal| (Example)
Free text
Any supplementary and personalised information relating to the person's goals, aims and hopes.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Goal importance score
Assessment of the person's motivations to achieve this goal, how important the goal is to the person on a scale of 1-10.
R 0…1
decimal
PLACEHOLDER — Numeric scale 1 - 10 (Example)
Actions and activities
Actions or activities the person or others plan to take to achieve the person's goals and the resources required to do this.

R 0…*
What
What the action is and how it is to be carried out?
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for the action.


R 0…1
SNOMED CT — 71388002 (Example)
Free text
Any supplementary and personalised information for the action.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)

Name and role (e.g. person, carer, GP, OT, etc.) of the person, or a team, carrying out the proposed action, and, if relevant where action should take place.
R 0…1
Name
The name of the professional or person carrying out the proposed action.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Role
The role of the person providing the service.
R 0…1
FHIR — sdsjobrole (Example)
Team
The name of the team.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Contact details

string
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
When

R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
Suggested strategies

text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text. (Example)

PLACEHOLDER — Numeric score 1 - 10 (Example)

datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time. (Example)
Stage goal

Contingency/safety plans

These are the things to do and people to contact, should an individual’s health or other circumstances get worse for safety.

R 0…*


Trigger factors
Signs to watch out for that may indicate a significant change in health or other circumstances e.g. feeling agitated
R 0…*
Coded value
The trigger that leads to instigation of a significant change in health or other circumstances.

R 0…1
SNOMED CT — SNOMED CT: - ^1127581000000103 | Health issues simple reference set (foundation metadata concept) | (Example)
Free text
Any free text associated with the person's trigger factor
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Contingency plan name
When the action evaluation needs to be reviewed. 
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for the type of care plan
R 0…1
SNOMED CT — SNOMED CT: - 999001471000000108 | Care plan type simple reference set (foundation metadata concept) | (Example)
Free text
Free text if no coded value is available.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
What should happen
Guidance on specific actions or interventions that may be required or should be avoided in specific situations. This may include circumstances where action needs to be taken if a carer is unable to care for the person. A statement of suggested actions. Usually expressed as: in the event of X do Y.
R 0…*
Coded value
The coded value for what should happen
R 0…1
SNOMED CT — 71388002 (Example)
Free text
Any supplementary or personalised information regarding what should happen
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Who should be contacted
Who should be contacted in the event of significant problems or deterioration in health or wellbeing. e.g. name, role and contact details.
R 0…*
Name
The name of the professional with responsibility for the care of the person.
R 0…1
text

Coping strategies
Details of coping strategies used
R 0…*
Coping strategy
The coping strategies are defined as thoughts and behaviours that people use to manage the internal and external demands of situations that are appraised as stressful. This should also include a record of any tools used to carry out the coping strategy.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Start date
The date when the coping strategy started
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
End date
The date when the coping strategy ended
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
Relapse indicators / early warning signs
Details of the relapse indicators for the person. These may also be called 'early warning signs'
R 0…*
PLACEHOLDER — (Example)

The coded value for relapse indicators/early warning signs

Additional support plans

R 0…*

The name of the particular additional supporting plan, e.g. dieticians plan, wound management plan, discharge management plan and behaviour support plan.
R 0…1
Coded value
The coded value for the type of care plan

text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Additional support plan content
This is the content of any additional care and support plan which the person and/or care professional consider should be shared with others providing care and support. May be structured in different ways, e.g. tables, diagrams, images. This is the content of any additional care and support plan which the individual and/or care professional consider should be shared with others providing care and support. It should be structured as recommended for the care and support plan and if contains additional detail, it may be referenced here.

End of life care

Information relating to end of life care.


N.B. This is not an end of life care plan but contains information that would be found in an end of life care plan.

R 0…1


Cardio-pulmonary resuscitation (CPR) decision
Whether a decision has been made, the decision, who made the decision, the date of decision, date for review and location of documentation. Where the person or their family member/carer have not been informed of the clinical decision please state the reason why.
R 0…1
Date
The date when the cardio-pulmonary resuscitation decision was made.

Priorities of care
The priorities agreed between the person and their health/care team, where the person has capacity: To get better; please consider all treatment to prolong life. To achieve a balance between getting better and ensuring good quality of life; please consider selected treatments. Comfort; please consider all treatments aimed at symptom control.

Anticipatory actions
Guidance on specific interventions or actions that may be required or should be avoided in specific situations. A statement of anticipatory actions. Usually expressed as: in the event of X do Y.

PLACEHOLDER — SCC11580 code : - 10: Hospital (Acute/Community/Other)20: Private residence21: Patient's own home22: Other private residence (eg relatives home, carers home)30: Hospice (inpatient specialist palliative care)40: Care Home50: Other (free text eg secure and detained settings)517111000000103, Preferred place of death: patient unable to express preference 517131000000106, Preferred place of death: discussion not appropriate766391000000108, Preferred place of death: patient declined discussion517161000000101, Preferred place of death: patient undecided (Example)

Documents (including correspondence, audio and images)

Details about documents related to the person.

R 0…1


Documents
This is the documents record entry. There may be 0 to many record entries under a section. Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items.
R 0…*
Date
The date and time the document was created.
R 0…1
datetime
PLACEHOLDER — Date and time (Example)
Performing professional
The professional who authored the document.

Documentation location
The location of the document
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text or URL (Example)
Confidentiality
The code specifying the level of confidentiality of the document.
R 0…1
FHIR — Confidentiality (Example)
Class
The document type e.g. report, summary, images, treatment plan, patient preferences, workflow
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text. (Example)
Document title
The title of the document.
R 0…1
text
PLACEHOLDER — Free text (Example)
Document name
The name of the document. This should align to the PRSB document naming standard. Where the document is a KOS document this field (designated typeCode) is used to carry the DICOM Imaging procedure: This attribute shall be populated by the XDS-I Imaging Document Source from a code in the Procedure Code Sequence (0008,1032) of the performed procedure with which the document is associated. Values may be found in a suitable DICOM browser
R 0…1
SNOMED CT — https://theprsb.org/wp-content/uploads/2018/11/Clinical-document-indexing-standards_NHS-Scotland.pdf (Example)
Document MIME type
MIME type of the document e.g. application, pdf, audio, jpeg
